keyword_data.research_summary: As of March 2026, the cybersecurity landscape has standardized on 'Autonomous Defense Fabrics' that collapse XDR, SASE, and IAM into unified, self-healing platforms. Industry data confirms a 70% enterprise adoption rate for FIDO2/WebAuthn passwordless authentication, which is now the mandatory compliance baseline. Critical developments include the shift to 'Continuous Identity Verification' (CIV) over static SSO, and AI-driven threat hunting utilities reducing MTTR by 45%. Organizations are now aggressively securing the 'Human-Machine' supply chain, applying strict Zero Trust governance to non-human service accounts, APIs, and CI/CD pipelines.
keywords: autonomous AI-driven XDR platforms for hybrid remote teams
zero trust identity-centric security utilities for 2026
self-healing security stacks for distributed enterprise workforces
keyword_data.selected: zero trust identity-centric security utilities for 2026

outline_data.title: Zero Trust Identity-Centric Security Utilities for 2026: The Autonomous Defense Fabric
outline_data.description: Discover how 2026 cybersecurity has evolved toward Autonomous Defense Fabrics with unified AI-driven XDR and SASE platforms. Explore the 70% adoption of FIDO2 passwordless authentication, Continuous Identity Verification (CIV) standards, and how AI-driven threat hunting reduces MTTR by 45%.
sections: Introduction: The Rise of Autonomous Defense Fabrics
The Convergence of XDR, SASE, and IAM
Mandatory Phishing-Resistant MFA: The FIDO2/WebAuthn Standard
Continuous Identity Verification (CIV): Moving Beyond Static SSO
AI-Driven Threat Hunting: Reducing MTTR by 45%
Governance for Service Accounts, APIs, and CI/CD Pipelines
Conclusion: Building the Autonomous Security Stack
